steps.title: Identify why compensation leads to favoring large donors
steps.content: The passage uses so to connect two ideas: charities can compensate donors for nuisance costs, and therefore they will favor large donors. The relationship between nuisance costs and donation size must explain why larger donations become preferable when costs are compensated.

steps.title: Apply the relationship to the economic logic
steps.content:
If nuisance costs are largely independent of donation size, the compensation a charity must pay does not scale with the size of the donation; reimbursing a \$10 donor can cost roughly as much as reimbursing a \$1000 donor.

That makes large donors more efficient targets when charities compensate donors for those costs.

The other three choices fail to create this economic incentive. That confirms C is correct.
